easy.title: Create a Commented HTML Template
easy.description: Create a clean HTML page template with comments throughout that explain each section. Include: comments identifying the DOCTYPE, head elements (charset, viewport, title), header, main content area, and footer. Use TODO comments to mark where content should be added. The comments should serve as documentation for a beginner learning HTML structure.
easy.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Commented Template</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Add comments to explain each section -->
</body>
</html>
easy.solution:
Welcome to My Website

Home About Contact

Hello, World!

This is my first HTML page with detailed comments.

About

This template demonstrates how to use HTML comments effectively.

Contact

Contact information goes here.

© 2026 My Website. All rights reserved.

medium.title: Build a Code Review Page with Comments
medium.description: Create an HTML page that demonstrates a 'code review' scenario. The page should contain a poorly written HTML section (with mistakes like unclosed tags, missing attributes, bad nesting) and use HTML comments above each mistake to explain what is wrong and how to fix it. Then include a 'corrected version' section. This simulates a code review process using comments as documentation.
medium.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Code Review with Comments</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a code review page with annotated mistakes -->
</body>
</html>
medium.solution:
HTML Code Review Demo

This page demonstrates a code review process using HTML comments to document issues.

Original Code (with issues)

Bad Code Example

This paragraph is missing a closing tag.

Outer paragraph

This nested paragraph is invalid

Block inside inline is not allowed
First
Second (duplicate id!)

Title

Should be h2




Section 2

Corrected Version

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Good Code Example</title>
</head>
<body>
    <img src="photo.jpg" alt="Description of photo">
    <p>This paragraph is properly closed.</p>
    <p>Each paragraph is a separate element.</p>
    <div>
        <span>Inline inside block is fine</span>
    </div>
    <div id="content1">First</div>
    <div id="content2">Second (unique ids)</div>
    <h1>Title</h1>
    <h2>Proper hierarchy</h2>
    <button disabled>Click (disabled)</button>
    <p style="margin-top: 20px;">CSS spacing instead of <br></p>
</body>
</html>

© 2026 Code Review Demo. Review by HTML Mastery Course.

hard.title: Build a 'Comment Your Code' Educational Page
hard.description: Create an interactive educational page that teaches the importance of HTML comments. Include: a section explaining what comments are, a section showing good vs bad comments (with real examples), a section on security (what NOT to comment), a section on comment conventions (TODO, FIXME, HACK, NOTE, REVIEW, SECTION), a section showing how to use comments during development to toggle features on/off, and a section on how minifiers handle comments. Each section should be clearly marked with section comments in the source code.
hard.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>How to Comment Your Code</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build an educational page about HTML comments -->
</body>
</html>
hard.solution:
How to Comment Your HTML Code

What | Good vs Bad | Security | Conventions | Toggling | Minification

What Are HTML Comments?

HTML comments are annotations in your code that are not displayed in the browser. They are visible in View Source (Ctrl+U).

Basic Syntax

<!-- This is a comment -->

Multi-line Comments

<!--
    This is a multi-line comment.
    It can span several lines.
-->

Good Comments vs Bad Comments

Bad Comments (Obvious)

<!-- This is a heading -->
<h1>Title</h1>

<!-- This is a paragraph -->
<p>Text</p>

Good Comments (Explain WHY)

<!-- HACK: Using table for layout because CSS grid is not supported in target browsers -->
<table>...</table>

<!-- NOTE: This section is intentionally hidden pending legal approval -->
<div hidden>...</div>

Security Warning

Never put sensitive information in HTML comments! Comments are visible to everyone who views the page source.

What NOT to put in comments:

  • ❌ Passwords and credentials
  • ❌ API keys and tokens
  • ❌ Database connection strings
  • ❌ Internal server paths
  • ❌ Personal user data (PII)
  • ❌ TODO items that reveal security issues

Bad Example (never do this):

<!-- ❌ DB Password: SuperSecret123! -->
<!-- ❌ API Key: sk-abcdef123456789 -->

Comment Conventions

Standard prefixes help communicate the purpose of comments:

Prefix Meaning Example
TODO Unfinished task <!-- TODO: Add pagination -->
FIXME Known bug <!-- FIXME: Image not loading on IE -->
HACK Temporary workaround <!-- HACK: Override for legacy browser -->
NOTE Important note <!-- NOTE: Requires PHP 8+ -->
REVIEW Needs review <!-- REVIEW: Is this approach optimal? -->
SECTION Section marker <!-- ===== HEADER ===== -->

Using Comments to Toggle Code

Comments are useful for temporarily disabling features during development:

<!-- Experiment A (disabled for now)
<div class="experiment-a">
    <p>New feature test</p>
</div>
-->

<!-- Experiment B (active) -->
<div class="experiment-b">
    <p>Current version</p>
</div>

Comments and Minification

HTML minifiers typically remove all comments to reduce file size. This is fine for production:

Before Minification After Minification
<!-- Header --><h1>Title</h1> <h1>Title</h1>

Use tools like HTMLMinifier, html-minifier-terser, or build tools like Webpack/Vite to strip comments automatically.

© 2026 HTML Mastery Course. <!-- You found the hidden comment! -->
